inline_comments.message:
Wierd inheritance for certificateWrappers.
Overally classes are the same - same implementation, only different constructors.
For me better way would be having two classes: CertificateWithAlias and CertificateWithAliasFactory having two methods - createPemCertificateWithAlias(Certificate certificate) and createCertificateWithAlias(Certificate certificate, String alias).
Would prefer if file was loaded once.
Would prefer if file was saved once.
Don't see a reason to provide keystoreType as constructor parameter. Can JksTruststore have different keystore types?

Not sure if this is correct implementation of adding certificates to pem file. As far as I know the file looks like:

 -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
 DATA BYTES
 -----END CERTIFICATE-----

wouldn't it just append certificates below the -----END CERTIFICATE-----?
Ok, that's how it should work.

I will prefer to, but I am afraid that Keystore instance not allow on it https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/security/KeyStore.html#load(java.io.InputStream,char%5B%5D).Documentation: "Note that if this keystore has already been loaded, it is reinitialized and loaded again from the given input stream." I think there is a risk of incorrect files loaded.

If we collect certificates from all file and try to save, and our logic detect alias conflict we haven't knowledge about which file contains conflicted alias. I think about situation if we have e.g. 10 files with 10 alias each. If we saving step by step and detect alias conflict we can see in logs which file contains conflicted alias.

Alias conflict doesn't happen during saving, so there is no difference for that. You would be calling addCertificates for each file and then saving it.

For me keystore should be initialized once in the begining, and loadFile should happen earlier - maybe in constructor.
Then all files should be appended, and then the base file should be saved.
Move this to factory, and keep only one constructor for CertificateWithAlias

Rename it because it serves both for key and trust stores. Also name do not describe what it does.
e.g. CertStoreManipulator could be better. Think of something even better.
I think its worth to make it public to allow calls from factory method for JksTruststore and P12Truststore.
Constructor that throws exceptions says as that is problem with design here. Why don't you pass it as a argument here and left all exception handling on factory level. Sounds better?

I think we dont need it. I would just log here with proper level that provided file extension is not supported.
We will have a bit shorter exceptions list here. 
Or second idea is to remain exception but change create  to trow "ExitableException"
@pbaniewski, others What do you think?
The same methods as in JksTruststore (== code duplication). I suggest to extract them to base class (e.g. JavaTruststore) and use it in both JKS and P12 :)
I don't see a logic which filters out private keys. Is this within BC implementation?

Logic is correct. We should report that unsupported type was provided. 
Up to you how you want to manage exceptions list
Switch doesn't ignore case so file with .JKS won't be recognized. Needs to be implemented in FileManager.
Tomasz you are repeating same code which leads to spaghetti code. Please look at getRawCertificates(File file) function and find common elements. Extract proper method and reuse just one.
We have created package for exceptions but not all of them are there. Why?
I moved all methods to common JavaTruststore
It is provided by Bouncy Castle Provider. I checked it and PRIVATE KEY is omit.
It is created by this MR. I moved all necessary exceptions to this package.

Consider moving wrap logic (with CertificateWithAliasFactory) to PemManipulator.

This way PemTruststore will be more similar to JavaTruststore when it comes to responsibility and dependencies, same with PemManipulator and KeystoreController.
Consider adding test case showing that when PEM contains key it is skipped in getCertificates()
wrong modifier order it should be static final, not final static.

potential error case. What will happen if more than one thread will invoke this method?
Method should be marked as synchronized or you should use AtomicInteger
wrong method name. Method which returns boolean should start from is,are or has

You make most visible in package browsing model classes which describe problem domain. This kind of files should lie somewhere deeper in package structure.
I propose to create package "model" and move there JavaTruststore, PemTruststore and Truststore.
Agree whats more I suggest to find some common naming. Since responsibilities are similar.
try to delegate as much as you can. Same to what is done in JavaTruststore.
Move with factory to so called PemManipulator.
Rename it to Truststore. Since this is wrapper class for Truststore.
I would rename it to simply get. No reason to be so verbosive here.

rename it to getFile(), we have doubled information here. When called now we have:
```
new TruststoreFile(file).getTrustoreFile()
```
looks weird.
Instance variables should be at the bottom.
move to the top (next line after class declaration)

instance variables should be at the bottom of the list.
Some general rules: https://www.oracle.com/java/technologies/javase/codeconventions-fileorganization.html
Missing logging in methods, check PemTruststore.java for them.
It not true. I would say: "Adding certificates for saving to file" or something similar.

Extract common interface with PemCertificateStoreController.java.
saveFile, getNotEmptyCertList ..., addCertificates

Add proper Exception as is depicted in graph: https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/DCAE+CertService+integration#DCAECertServiceintegration-Option1(DCAEextrainitcontainer(akatrustmerger))
Make common naming with PemCertificateStoreController.java.
Get rid of those empty lines.
Consider exporting these constants into the class or enum with public access modifier to use them in entire TrustStoreMerger project.
Consider to place it on top.
This string constant occurs many times in the project, consider putting it in one place.

please add javadocs on all new classes and public methods

(and if possible, improve javadocs on methods that are modified)
